answer:
Most YouTube Transcript API services authenticate requests using a Bearer token provided in the Authorization header [1][2][3][4][5]. You must include your API key in the format Bearer YOUR_API_KEY [2][6][3].

Example request:

```bash
curl -X POST https://www.youtubetranscript.dev/api/v2/transcribe \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"video": "VIDEO_ID"}'
```

While most services use this standard Bearer scheme [1][2][3][4][5], at least one provider (Scority) requires an x-api-key header instead [7]. Always check the specific documentation for your chosen provider to confirm their header requirement.
